name: Prone position
description: Prone (face-down) body position — the position state, not the act of placing the patient prone.

en.name: Prone position
en.shortDescription: Prone (face-down) body position — the position state, not the act of placing the patient prone.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Prone position is the body position in which the patient lies face-down (on the stomach). In intensive care, placing a patient prone improves oxygenation in severe ARDS and is a standard intervention there. In source data this set captures the **position state** — that the patient is/was in the prone position — as an observation, rather than the action of turning the patient prone. There is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia.

## Included Concepts

- *4050473 / 1240000 — Prone body position (SNOMED)*: the patient is in the prone (face-down) body position.

## Mapping Notes

- This concept captures the **state** (the patient is prone), not the act of placing the patient prone. Source records of the *procedure* 'placing subject in prone position' or 'assisted ventilation in prone position' describe the action/intervention; map the resulting position state here.
- **Not semi-prone / lateral**: the semi-prone and lateral positions are distinct body positions and are not part of this set.

fr.name: Décubitus ventral
fr.shortDescription: Décubitus ventral (face vers le bas) — l’état de position, non le geste de mise en décubitus ventral.

versions.message: Capture the position STATE: replace "Placing subject in prone position" (4196006, the action) with "Prone body position" (4050473, the state Observation); rename set to "Prone position". Added description.
